sourceMovie.title: The Butterfly Effect
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Butterfly Effect (2004) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Butterfly Effect clicked for you, The Island (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Frequency
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Butterfly Effect clicked for you, Frequency (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Butterfly Effect?
faq.answer: Try The Island, Frequency, The Endless—each shares DNA with The Butterfly Effect while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Butterfly Effect?
faq.answer: The Butterfly Effect (2004) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Butterfly Effect part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Butterfly Effect Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
